sections.name: Corrosive Lizard

link.alt: Pathfinder #151: The Show Must Go On pg. 45
link.name: Pathfinder #151: The Show Must Go On pg. 45

sources.name: Pathfinder #151: The Show Must Go On

sections.text: This creature did not include a description.

link.alt: Pathfinder #151: The Show Must Go On pg. 45
link.name: Pathfinder #151: The Show Must Go On pg. 45

sources.name: Pathfinder #151: The Show Must Go On

traits.name: Uncommon
traits.text: Something of uncommon rarity requires special training or comes from a particular culture or part of the world. Some character choices give access to uncommon options, and the GM can choose to allow access for anyone. Less is known about uncommon creatures than common creatures. They typically can't be summoned. The DC of Recall Knowledge checks related to these creature is increased by 2.

traits.name: Evil
traits.text: Evil effects often manipulate energy from evil-aligned Outer Planes and are antithetical to good divine servants or divine servants of good deities. A creature with this trait is evil in alignment.

traits.name: Animal
traits.text: An animal is a creature with a relatively low intelligence. It typically doesn’t have an Intelligence ability modifier over –4, can’t speak languages, and can’t be trained in Intelligence-based skills.

traits.name: Fiend
traits.text: Creatures that hail from or have a strong connection to the evil-aligned planes are called fiends. Fiends can survive the basic environmental effects of planes in the Outer Sphere.

automatic_abilities.effect: The lizard's jaws deal 1d6 piercing damage and 1d6 acid damage to the triggering creature.
automatic_abilities.name: Gnashing Bite   Trigger A creature grabbed by the corrosive lizard's jaws fails a check to Escape

ability.name: Gout of Acid
ability.text: The corrosive lizard vomits a gout of acid in a 15-foot cone. Each creature in this area takes 3d6 acid damage (DC 20 basic Reflex save). The lizard can't use Gout of Acid again for 1d4 rounds.

traits.name: Acid
traits.text: Effects with this trait deal acid damage. Creatures with this trait have a magical connection to acid.

traits.name: Primal
traits.text: This magic comes from the primal tradition, connecting to the natural world and instinct. Anything with this trait is magical.

ability.name: Lurching Charge
ability.text: The corrosive lizard Strides up to double its Speed and then makes a jaws Strike. If the lizard moved at least 20 feet away from its starting position, it gains a +2 circumstance bonus to this attack roll.

universal_monster_ability.name: Darkvision
universal_monster_ability.text: A monster with darkvision can see perfectly well in areas of darkness and dim light, though such vision is in black and white only. Some forms of magical darkness, such as a 4th-level *darkness* spell, block normal darkvision. A monster with greater darkvision, however, can see through even these forms of magical darkness.

universal_monster_ability.name: Scent
universal_monster_ability.text:
Scent involves sensing creatures or objects by smell, and is usually a vague sense. The range is listed in the ability, and it functions only if the creature or object being detected emits an aroma (for instance, incorporeal creatures usually do not exude an aroma).  
  
 If a creature emits a heavy aroma or is upwind, the GM can double or even triple the range of scent abilities used to detect that creature, and the GM can reduce the range if a creature is downwind.
